question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a non-network-based DoS by causing the application to enter the retry loop in waitForDatabase() indefinitely, blocking the Spring DataSource bean initialization and preventing the entire mirror node service from starting, given that the database is kept unreachable through local resource exhaustion (e.g., filling the DB host's disk), causing the service to remain unavailable for the full timeout duration?
